steps.title: Contrast two waste categories
steps.content: The first example is post-consumer waste because the brochure became waste after reaching the consumer. The next example is pre-consumer waste because paper scraps became waste before reaching the consumer. The transition should mark this opposition.

steps.title: Reject example and result links
steps.content: Choice A would introduce another example of the same idea, but the category changes. Choice B would narrow or specify the previous idea, which the sentence does not do. Choice D signals a result, but paper scraps are not caused by the thrown-away brochure. The contrast transition fits. The answer is C.
